data.body: PASS 1 (T1 prior-art/novel-architecture overlay) — coaching note: AC#1 calls for reconciler coordination to be "preserved or redesigned" once `.bridge_state/` + lock/gate files leave the shared `tickets` branch, but the plan does not consider prior art for how off-branch cross-clone coordination would be achieved. Today's mechanism (a committed-file advisory lock advanced via a `git update-ref` CAS on `refs/heads/tickets`, plus a committed phase-gate file read with `git show tickets:`) IS the cross-clone coordination channel; removing those files from history removes the very channel they replace. The "design pass" is acknowledged but deferred without surveying the established options (e.g. dedicated git refs/notes namespaces, a separate coordination branch, ref-CAS-only locks without a committed payload). Recommend the plan name the candidate off-branch coordination mechanism(s) and weigh them against the existing CAS-ref approach before committing, so the redesign is not reinvented from scratch in the follow-up.
